description_long: This is a classic claw hammer featuring a straight, cylindrical wooden handle with a natural brown finish. The head is made of dark metal and consists of a flat striking face on one side and a curved, bifurcated claw on the other, designed for pulling nails. The transition between the handle and the head is reinforced, with the wood fitting securely into the metal head. The hammer's overall proportions suggest it is suitable for one-handed use, typical for carpentry or general repair tasks. The surface of the handle appears smooth and polished, while the metal head has a matte, slightly textured finish. No visible branding, wear, or additional attachments are present.
description: A traditional claw hammer with a smooth wooden handle and a dark metal head featuring a flat face and a curved claw. The tool appears clean and well-crafted, with no visible markings or damage.
description_view_0: The hammer is shown at an angle, highlighting the full length of the wooden handle and the side profile of the metal head, including both the striking face and the claw.
description_view_1: This top-down view emphasizes the bifurcated claw and the circular end of the handle, showing the alignment of the head and handle.
description_view_2: The side view displays the straight wooden handle and the profile of the hammer head, with the claw and striking face both visible.
description_view_3: The hammer is viewed from the opposite angle, focusing on the handle's length and the underside of the metal head, with the claw's curvature clearly visible.
attribute_handle_type: straight wooden handle, cylindrical, smooth finish, no visible grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: claw hammer head, dark metal, with a flat striking face and a curved, bifurcated claw for nail removal
